title: Chhattisgarh announces early school summer vacation amid heatwave - EdexLive
full_text:
Chhattisgarh government prepones summer vacation for schools across the state, due to the ongoing heatwave conditions. The decision was taken following a review of the prevailing weather patterns, with several districts witnessing temperatures exceeding 40 degrees celsius.
As per the official order by state school education department, school summer holidays are set to begin from April 20, till June 15 for all government, aided, non-aided and private schools — advancing the break by nearly two weeks from the originally scheduled date of May 1.
However, the revised order will not apply to teachers.
Speaking about the move on X, Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai said, "Ensuring the safety of children during the severe heatwave is our top priority. The summer vacation has been preponed so that their health is not adversely affected. Summer vacation has now been declared from April 20 to June 15, thereby ensuring the safety and well-being of children amidst this scorching heat."
As per Meteorological Centre in Raipur, heatwave conditions are likely to prevail in isolated pockets of Bilaspur, Raipur and Durg divisions between April 16 and April 19. With maximum temperature expected to rise by 1-3 degrees celsius over the next three days.

title: Heatwave Alert in Chhattisgarh, Temperatures Cross 44°C - Dainik Jagran MP CG
full_text:
Heatwave Alert in Chhattisgarh, Temperatures Cross 44°C
Raipur (CG)
Heatwave in Chhattisgarh intensifies as temperatures hit 44°C, with alerts issued and precautions advised to stay safe during extreme heat.
Raipur Weather Update: Chhattisgarh continues to experience severe heat conditions, with no immediate relief expected in the coming days. The weather department has issued a heatwave alert for the next two days, particularly affecting regions under Raipur, Bilaspur, and Durg divisions. Residents are being advised to remain cautious as temperatures remain significantly above normal levels.
Temperatures Cross 44°C in Rajnandgaon
In the last 24 hours, Rajnandgaon recorded the highest temperature in the state at 44°C, making it one of the hottest locations in the region. At the same time, Ambikapur registered the lowest temperature at 21°C, indicating a sharp contrast between day and night temperatures. Despite relatively cooler nights, the daytime heat remains intense and uncomfortable.
Four Cities Record Over 42°C
At least four cities across the state have reported temperatures exceeding 42°C. This persistent rise in temperature highlights the growing intensity of the summer season. The absence of cloud cover and rainfall has further aggravated the situation, allowing heat levels to build up rapidly during the day.
Dry Weather with No Rainfall Activity
The current weather pattern across Chhattisgarh remains completely dry. According to meteorological officials, there is no active weather system influencing the region at present. As a result, dry and hot conditions are expected to continue over the next couple of days without any signs of rainfall.
Advisory Issued for Public Safety
Authorities have urged residents to take necessary precautions to avoid heat-related illnesses. People are advised to avoid stepping out during peak afternoon hours and to stay hydrated throughout the day. Wearing light clothing and limiting outdoor activities can help reduce the risk of heatstroke and dehydration.
Raipur Weather Update
The capital city Raipur is expected to witness clear skies throughout the day. The maximum temperature is likely to hover around 43°C, while the minimum may stay close to 27°C. These conditions will contribute to a prolonged period of heat, making daytime activities challenging for residents.
